title: A negated 'Any' query should be written as 'All'
messageFormat: Express this negated 'Any' call as an 'All' call with the opposite condition.
summary: Negating Any reads as "there is no element that matches", which is the same as "every element fails the condition". Writing it as All with the inverted predicate makes that universal statement explicit.
Both expressions evaluate identically; the change only clarifies what the query is really asserting.
